items.mistake: Expecting a single perceptron to learn XOR or other non-separable tasks
items.fix: Verify linear separability first; use an MLP with hidden layers, kernel SVM, or explicit feature engineering for nonlinear structure.
items.why: One hyperplane cannot split XOR in 2D — this is a representational limit, not a tuning problem.
items.example: Perceptron on XOR tops out at 75% accuracy (always wrong on one point). MLPClassifier(hidden_layer_sizes=(4,)) reaches 100%.

items.mistake: Forgetting the bias term when data is not centered at the origin
items.fix: Always include b in z = wᵀx + b; without bias, the boundary is forced through the origin and may be infeasible even when classes are separable.
items.why: Separating hyperplanes may require offset from zero — e.g. AND needs b = −1.5 with w = (1,1).
items.example: With b fixed at 0, AND labels on unit square corners may be impossible to separate; adding learnable b fixes it.

items.mistake: Training on unscaled features with mixed units
items.fix: Standardize continuous inputs so weight updates and convergence are balanced across dimensions.
items.why: Large-magnitude features dominate the dot product and learning rule updates.
items.example: Income in thousands vs age in decades — StandardScaler before Perceptron stabilizes η-effective updates.

items.fix: Use step output as a hard class label; switch to logistic regression or apply calibration if probabilities are required.
items.why: Step activation has no meaningful scale between 0 and 1 — only side of the hyperplane matters.
items.example: Wrong: score z = 0.01 interpreted as 1% confidence. Right: ŷ = 1 because z ≥ 0.
